slides.title: SEARCH Updates on SS Exit Signal Quality and Draining Phase
Firefox QUIC Slow Start Experiments (Classic/SEARCH/HyStart++) v2 with bonus plots

polls.text: I have read a version of draft-chung-ccwg-search
I have implemented or am planning to implement a version of SEARCH

chat.author: Reese Enghardt
chat.text: Hi everyone! We'll get started in a few minutes

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: If queueing is out of scope for CCWG I'm not sure why we're having this presentation

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: Oh I guess there's non-queueing stuff

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: I love this integration of picoquic in NS3! And yes, there is no plan to change the public APIs that the integration in NS3 uses. The policy is to maintain old APIs, possibly changing their implementation to use new ones.

chat.author: Marco Munizaga
chat.text: I want to highlight the Shadow project: https://github.com/shadow/shadow also a network simulator that implements the linux syscall boundary. I've used it to run simulations with quic-go.

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: C4 has been doing the equivalent of precautionary probing from the start!

chat.author: Reese Enghardt
chat.text: @Meetecho Camera on speaker, please

chat.author: altanai
chat.text: +1 on the camera request

chat.author: Eric Kinnear
chat.text: @Meetecho our front audience mic claims its out of battery, can we get some fresh ones please?

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: @Eric Kinnear ack, I'll notify the AV team

chat.author: Eric Kinnear
chat.text: Thank you! If there are some in the room already, happy to swap them in

chat.author: altanai
chat.text: just fyi the source code for the screamv2 draft (https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-ccwg-rfc8298bis-screamv2/)  is unreachable at the github repo mention in the draft https://github.com/gloinul/draft-johansson-ccwg-scream-bis

chat.author: Ian Swett
chat.text: +1 to Neal and Stuart on Policers and not adding special code.

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: scone?

chat.author: Ian Swett
chat.text: For Magnus: What does happen with Screamv2 if you have a giant file transfer that's never app-limited?

chat.author: Eric Kinnear
chat.text: Scone seems topical for the policer part, for sure

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: scone isn't too relevant for screamv2 until it's on quic tho

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: And there's no reason it couldn't be but right now we're using it for webrtc

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: (The Google tests)

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: @Altani the draft exist here, I don't know why the submitted draft references my my github user repository: https://github.com/IngJohEricsson/draft-johansson-ccwg-scream-bis

chat.author: Craig Taylor
chat.text: Can remotes see the presentation? Not seeing on the onsite tool

chat.author: Reese Enghardt
chat.text: I can see the presentation being remote

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: It's a screenshare not a PDF - onsite doesn't show screenshare, since it's a video

chat.author: Jason Livingood
chat.text: Presenter should RAISE his voice a bit if possible.

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: You can use the full tool onsite, just turn off audio playback

chat.author: Jason Livingood
chat.text: thx1

chat.author: Jason Livingood
chat.text: thx!

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: @Ian to my understanding what happens if you have unlimited data is that as long as you are not congested you will be allowed to send the ref_win_overhead times ref_win and this will be paced. But as it is ack clocked you will from having commited that amount of data stay at ref window increase and the ack clocking. See https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-ietf-ccwg-rfc8298bis-screamv2-01.html#name-sender-transmission-control-2

chat.author: Ian Swett
chat.text: Thanks Magnus

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: But, I think it is something that we should evaluate showing that Scream is not bad behaved in this case.

chat.author: Gorry Fairhurst
chat.text: Persoannly, I agree with Christian, than any signal to move to CA has a huge impact on a high BDP path can be very bad - and do hope we can be very conservative to what triggers some behaviour.

chat.author: Eric Kinnear
chat.text: @Martin -- we'd previous done an adoption call for SEARCH and gotten some great feedback on the list for the authors that they're addressing now as well as the experiments that we see presented during this talk. So not today asking about adoption (Standards track or otherwise), but we may choose as a WG to adopt a document in this space in the future.

chat.author: Gorry Fairhurst
chat.text: I missed the context - is this firefox data for download  or upload (assuming these are broadband customer sites?)

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: If this is FF's own congestion controller I guess it must be upload?

chat.author: Craig Taylor
chat.text: I think he said that on earlier slide

chat.author: Eric Kinnear
chat.text: A prior slide did say uploads, so I'm guessing this one also is?

chat.author: Mohit Tahiliani
chat.text: Yes, it is for uploads

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: FF doesn't have control of downloads nor indeed can it definitively (non-heruistically) tell when the remote sender exited slowstart

chat.author: altanai
chat.text:
+1 to the question just asked 
Before comparing Classic, SEARCH, and HyStart, can the results separate exits caused by loss/ECN congestion events so each algorithm is evaluated only on comparable slow-start opportunities?

chat.author: Gorry Fairhurst
chat.text: I'm wondering about upload bandwidth varying - and expect would there be few flows sharing an uplink bottleneck?

chat.author: Ian Swett
chat.text: I'm happy to talk later, but I want to hear the last two talks and we're low on time.

chat.author: Oskar Mansfeld
chat.text: To answer some of the chat questions: Yes this is Firefox's sending path, i.e. uploads from Firefox, using our Congestion Controller

chat.author: Oskar Mansfeld
chat.text: @altanai: With the way we had set up the metrics for this experiment we unfortunately can't filter the ssthresh at slow start exit for loss/ecn exit and heuristic exits, only per cohort. Looking back we should have included that, but when we thought of it it was already too late. We will rework those metrics for future work.

chat.author: altanai
chat.text: Thanks i will look forward to it in IETF 127 @ Oskar
